question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter) in SharedTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user set prefetch to its maximum (256 via ListenerProperties) by manipulating configuration, causing the publishOn(Schedulers.boundedElastic(), false, prefetch) to pre-fetch 256 messages per subscriber, exhausting the bounded elastic thread pool and causing message delivery to stall for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isNext(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isNext() check in TopicContext which only compares sequenceNumber == last.getSequenceNumber() + 1, such that if the live stream delivers a message with sequenceNumber = last + 2 (skipping one), the onNext() method throws IllegalStateException and terminates the stream, causing all subsequent messages to be missing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: missingMessages(TopicContext, TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the duplicate message detection in missingMessages() where numMissingMessages <= -1 causes Flux.empty() to be returned, such that if the live stream re-delivers a message with a lower sequence number than last, the message is silently dropped even if it is a legitimate correction, causing incorrect records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toResponse(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user receive a ConsensusTopicResponse where runningHashVersion defaults to DEFAULT_RUNNING_HASH_VERSION=3 when the stored value is null, causing the exported record to contain an incorrect runningHashVersion that does not match the actual consensus state, misleading clients about message integrity?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: parseTransactionID(byte[], long, long)] Can an unprivileged external user receive a ConsensusTopicResponse where parseTransactionID() silently returns null on InvalidProtocolBufferException (only logging an error), causing the chunkInfo's initialTransactionID to be omitted from the exported record, resulting in an incomplete/incorrect chunk record being delivered?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user submit messages with a consensusTimestamp set far in the future, causing recordMetric() to compute a negative latency (System.currentTimeMillis() - consensusTimestamp/1000000 < 0) which is silently discarded by the latency > 0 guard, masking the anomaly and preventing operators from detecting incorrect timestamp records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter) in CompositeTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that when listenerProperties.isEnabled() returns false, listen() returns Flux.empty() with no error, causing the subscriber to silently receive zero records with no indication that the listener is disabled, resulting in missing records with no observable error?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a misconfigured ListenerType (e.g., an unknown type) to cause getTopicListener() to throw UnsupportedOperationException, which propagates through the reactive chain and terminates all active subscriptions, causing all subscribers to lose their message streams simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: poll(PollingContext) in PollingTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with a very large limit value causing the pageSize calculation Math.min(limit, listenerProperties.getMaxPageSize()) to always return maxPageSize, resulting in the poll loop never terminating and continuously re-querying the database, starving other subscribers of database resources and causing their message delivery to be delayed or missing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByFilter(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TOPIC_MESSAGES_BY_ID_QUERY_HINT native query (set local random_page_cost = 0) executed via entityManager.createNativeQuery().executeUpdate() for every non-limit-1 query, causing session-level planner cost changes that affect concurrent queries in the same transaction/session and lead to suboptimal query plans that miss or incorrectly order records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 1-second safety check delay (Mono.delay(Duration.ofSeconds(1L))) in TopicMessageServiceImpl where the safetyCheck Flux only fires if !topicContext.isComplete(), such that if the topic has an endTime set to less than 1 second in the future, the safety check never fires and any messages missed during the historical-to-live transition are permanently lost?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isValidStartTime()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a startTime equal to exactly DomainUtils.now() at the moment of validation, which passes isValidStartTime() (startTime <= DomainUtils.now()), but by the time the listener starts polling, the startTime is in the past by several milliseconds, causing the first poll interval to miss messages that arrived in that gap?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter) in PollingTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the delaySubscription(interval, scheduler) in PollingTopicListener which delays the first poll by the full interval (default 500ms), causing messages that arrive in the first 500ms after subscription to be missed if they are not covered by the historical retriever's endTime?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe(Topic) in RedisTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .share() operator on the Redis subscription Flux, which multicasts to all subscribers but drops messages for slow subscribers who have not yet requested items, causing some subscribers to miss messages that were published before they attached to the shared Flux?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSharedListener(TopicMessageFilter) in RedisTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic, causing computeIfAbsent() to create a new shared Flux, then immediately cancel, triggering unsubscribe() which removes the entry, then re-subscribe rapidly in a loop to repeatedly create and destroy the shared subscription, causing a window of missing messages for all other subscribers each time the channel is torn down?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toFilter(ConsensusTopicQuery)] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery without a topicID field (hasTopicID() = false), causing toFilter() to build a TopicMessageFilter with a null topicId, which then fails validation with a NullPointerException or returns messages for all topics, resulting in incorrect records being exported?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toFilter(ConsensusTopicQuery)] Can an unprivileged external user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with both consensusStartTime and consensusEndTime set to the same nanosecond value, bypassing the isValidEndTime() check (endTime > startTime is false), causing a validation error that silently drops the subscription rather than returning an appropriate error, resulting in zero records exported?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter, boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the unthrottled retrieval path in PollingTopicMessageRetriever (throttled=false) by being the first subscriber after a gap, causing maxPolls=12 polls at 20ms intervals to be exhausted before all missing messages are retrieved, resulting in permanently missing records in the gap-fill export?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isComplete() in PollingContext] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isComplete() logic in PollingTopicMessageRetriever where throttled=true returns true when pageSize < maxPageSize, such that if the last page happens to be exactly one message short of maxPageSize due to a concurrent deletion, the retriever stops early and misses remaining messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user send a subscription with a topicId that maps to a valid entity of type ACCOUNT (not TOPIC), causing topicExists() to return Mono.error(new IllegalArgumentException('Not a valid topic')), which is mapped to a gRPC status error and silently terminates the stream, but the error message leaks internal entity type information?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribeTopic(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.merge(safetyCheck, live) in TopicMessageServiceImpl where both the safety check and the live stream may independently retrieve the same missing messages, causing duplicate records to be emitted before the sequence number deduplication filter (t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()) removes them, resulting in incorrect ordering?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: pastEndTime(TopicContext)] Can an unprivileged external user set endTime to a value in the past (but still > startTime to pass validation), causing pastEndTime() to immediately complete the stream via the RepeatSpec check, but the historical retriever may still be mid-stream, causing an abrupt truncation of the historical record export?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onNext(TopicMessage) in TopicContext] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the IllegalStateException in TopicContext.onNext() by causing out-of-order message delivery (e.g., via Redis message reordering), which terminates the entire subscriber stream and causes all subsequent messages to be missing, with no automatic recovery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter) in SharedTopicListener] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the publishOn(Schedulers.boundedElastic(), false, prefetch) call where the second argument (delayError=false) means errors are propagated immediately, causing a single error from one subscriber's processing to terminate the shared downstream pipeline and drop messages for all other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findByFilter(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a filter with endTime set to a value less than startTime that somehow passes the isValidEndTime() check (e.g., due to a clock skew between validation and query execution), causing the JPA predicate cb.lessThan(consensusTimestamp, endTime) to return an empty result set and export zero records?
